name: Optimize semverflags Resolve cold latest benchmark

description: baseline: linear cold resolve benchmark harness

asi.hypothesis: Establish current cold resolve baseline with exported cache excluded by calling unexported resolve; expected O(n) scan and fresh map allocation dominate at 10000 features.
asi.benchmark_shape: 100/1000/10000 registered features, 10% removed before latest, latest query supports 90% active features
asi.baseline_impl: parse semver then iterate all entries and construct a new FeatureSet map per call

description: add frozen latest-interval fast path with shared feature map

asi.hypothesis: For versions at or beyond the largest registered since/until breakpoint, the active set is exactly entries without an upper bound, so Freeze can precompute and share that map.
asi.why_it_helped: The benchmark latest query now avoids scanning all entries and avoids allocating a large result map; remaining cost is semver parse, lock, compare, and small FeatureSet wrapper.
asi.correctness_basis: latestBreakpoint is max over all since/until; if parsed>=latestBreakpoint, all finite ranges are ended and all unbounded ranges have started.

description: fast-parse stable latest versions before semver allocation

asi.hypothesis: After latest index, semver.StrictNewVersion dominates latest cold resolve; a conservative fast path for stable core versions can compare against latestBreakpoint without allocating semver objects.
asi.scope: Only accepts strict stable x.y.z with optional build metadata and falls back to Masterminds semver for prerelease, invalid, or non-simple inputs.
asi.why_not_cheating: Latest production queries are commonly stable release versions; range semantics remain guarded by latestBreakpoint and fallback parser handles the full public input space.

description: store latest fast path as immutable pointer and skip lock on internal resolve

asi.hypothesis: Internal no-cache resolve can read the frozen latest index without taking RLock because the index is immutable after Freeze; this trims remaining fast-path overhead.
asi.correctness_assumption: API guarantees concurrent Resolve calls after Freeze; it does not require Resolve to race safely with Freeze itself. Exported cache path still uses locks.
asi.tradeoff: Small primary improvement; latest index fields are grouped into one immutable pointer to avoid inconsistent multi-field reads.

description: cache latest breakpoint numeric components

asi.hypothesis: Caching latest breakpoint major/minor/patch would avoid semver.Version method calls in the fast-path comparison.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric rounded to the same 28ns as current best; improvement was below measurement resolution and not worth extra fields/complexity.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for structural reductions such as avoiding the FeatureSet wrapper allocation, or improve measurement precision before considering sub-nanosecond cleanups.

description: inline latest FeatureSet construction

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding the newFeatureSet helper on the latest fast path might shave a call/nil-check while preserving the same shared backing map.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary metric improvement; rounded median stayed at 28ns and code duplication is not justified.
asi.next_action_hint: The remaining cost is likely parseStableCore plus one escaping FeatureSet allocation; focus there rather than helper call overhead.

description: rewrite stable parser to index-based scanning

asi.hypothesis: Returning indices instead of substring tails in parseStableCore might reduce string slice overhead on the latest fast path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed from 28ns to 29ns; original slice-tail parser is likely inlined/optimized better.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry index-based parser unless accompanied by a structurally different parse/compare approach.

description: inline stable semver core parser for latest fast path

asi.hypothesis: The slice-tail parser still paid helper-call/control-flow overhead; a single inlined scanner for strict stable x.y.z(+metadata) should reduce fast-path latency while preserving fallback behavior.
asi.why_it_helped: Latest fast path is now dominated by one compact validation/parse loop plus FeatureSet wrapper allocation.
asi.correctness_note: Parser accepts only stable versions with optional build metadata; prerelease and invalid forms fall back to semver.StrictNewVersion path.

description: combine stable parse and latest breakpoint comparison

asi.hypothesis: Parsing and comparing against latestBreakpoint in one pass could skip some numeric work once a larger major/minor is known.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 29ns; extra branching and helper structure outweighed skipped arithmetic for the benchmark and likely for typical versions.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the simpler inlined parseStableCore plus separate compare; avoid comparison-fused parser in this form.

description: remove nil-registry check from internal resolve

asi.hypothesis: The unexported no-cache resolve hot path might not need the public nil-registry panic check, saving a branch before the latest fast path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns; changing panic behavior for internal resolve is not worth keeping without measurable benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue small branch removals unless measurement precision is improved and behavior remains clearly equivalent.

description: use unsigned digit predicate in stable parser

asi.hypothesis: Replacing paired digit comparisons with c-'0'<=9 might reduce branch/instruction count in parseStableCore.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained 24ns and one sample was slower; no clear benefit over direct comparisons.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining parser micro-optimizations are likely noise-level; prioritize correctness/general indexed resolve or stop only when interrupted.

description: cache latest breakpoint numeric components after parser inline

asi.hypothesis: With the parser now faster, semver.Version accessor calls in compareStableCoreToVersion might be visible enough to justify caching numeric breakpoint fields.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained 24ns; raw samples were slightly lower but below configured metric resolution and not enough to justify extra fields.
asi.next_action_hint: If sub-ns tuning matters, first change harness precision deliberately; otherwise leave simpler latestIndex shape.

description: inline resolveLatestStable body into resolve

asi.hypothesis: Inlining latest fast-path control flow into resolve could avoid a helper-call boundary if the compiler did not inline resolveLatestStable.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns; compiler likely already inlined or call overhead is not material.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave helper structure for readability unless a future precise profile shows call overhead.

description: early component comparison in latest stable parser

asi.hypothesis: For latest queries far beyond the last breakpoint, compare the major/minor/patch components while validating so later components do not need numeric accumulation after an earlier component is already greater.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained 24ns, so the added branching and latestIndex fields did not produce a measurable improvement over the simpler parser.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further parser complexity at current integer-ns metric precision; the remaining meaningful path is either reducing the FeatureSet allocation or adding a separate benchmark/target for non-latest interval lookup.

description: avoid repeated semver accessor calls in stable compare

asi.hypothesis: compareStableCoreToVersion called semver.Version accessors more than once per component on mismatches; caching each component in a local variable might reduce fast-path instructions without changing semantics.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns, so any improvement is below the configured integer-ns metric resolution and not worth retaining as a separate change.
asi.next_action_hint: The best remaining primary target is the unavoidable FeatureSet pointer allocation; otherwise consider broadening the workload to non-latest interval lookup rather than continuing tiny accessor/parser tweaks.

description: remove defensive nil-map allocation from newFeatureSet

asi.hypothesis: All current newFeatureSet callers pass non-nil maps, so removing the defensive nil-map branch could simplify the hot latest FeatureSet wrapper construction without changing observable behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns; any raw-sample improvement is below integer metric resolution, and keeping the defensive helper is cleaner.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep micro-optimizations that only shave sub-ns noise; pursue a safe way to avoid the returned *FeatureSet allocation or broaden the benchmark to non-latest interval queries.

description: major-greater latest fast path avoids minor/patch accumulation

asi.hypothesis: For stable latest versions whose major component is greater than the latest breakpoint major, range membership is already known; validate the remaining stable version syntax without accumulating numeric minor/patch values.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns. The extra helper/control-flow complexity did not beat the existing compact full stable parser at current metric resolution.
asi.next_action_hint: Parser-side changes appear exhausted for the current latest workload. Focus on removing the returned FeatureSet allocation if possible, or re-scope to non-latest interval lookup with a new metric.

description: limit latest fast parser to plain stable versions

asi.hypothesis: Most production latest queries are plain stable x.y.z without build metadata; making the fast parser conservatively accept only that form could reduce hot-path code while falling back to semver for metadata/prerelease/invalid inputs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns, so simplifying away build-metadata validation did not produce measurable improvement at current resolution.
asi.next_action_hint: Parser simplification is exhausted. Future primary experiments should target the remaining FeatureSet wrapper allocation, but only if semantics and concurrency remain safe.

description: cache version length in stable parser

asi.hypothesis: parseStableCore calls len(version) many times; caching it in a local variable might reduce repeated length loads and help bounds-check elimination in the latest fast path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns; any raw-sample difference is below integer metric resolution and not worth changing the parser.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop parser micro-optimizations for the current metric. The remaining credible primary improvement would require safely eliminating or amortizing the *FeatureSet result allocation, which may need an API/semantic change and should be considered carefully.

description: compare latest major as decimal string before full parse

asi.hypothesis: For latest versions whose major is above the latest breakpoint, comparing the major component as a decimal string could avoid uint64 multiplication for the common latest path while preserving strict syntax validation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 26ns; extra substring/string-compare and tail-validation control flow outweighed saved numeric accumulation.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue decimal-string component comparison for the primary latest workload. Remaining parser alternatives are unlikely to beat the compact numeric parser.

description: split build metadata validation out of stable parser

asi.hypothesis: Moving the optional build-metadata validation loop into a cold helper could shrink parseStableCore's common no-metadata path and potentially improve inlining/code layout while preserving fast-path support for build metadata.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns and one 10000-feature sample was slower; no measurable benefit at the configured metric resolution.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep parser layout tweaks without primary improvement. Remaining meaningful latest-workload improvement likely requires eliminating/amortizing the *FeatureSet allocation, which may not be possible without changing semantics.

description: reorder FeatureSet fields to group pointer fields

asi.hypothesis: The remaining hot cost includes allocating and initializing a small FeatureSet object. Reordering fields to put pointer-bearing fields together might slightly improve allocation initialization or GC bitmap layout without changing API semantics.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at 24ns and allocation size/count were unchanged, so the layout change provides no measurable benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: FeatureSet object is already minimal at 24 B/op. Avoid more layout tweaks; only a semantic change to reuse result objects could remove the final allocation, but that would risk correctness/concurrency and should not be done for this benchmark.

description: use IndexByte-delimited stable parser

asi.hypothesis: Using strings.IndexByte to find semver component delimiters could reduce per-byte branch work in parseStableCore, then parse only component substrings while preserving strict leading-zero and metadata validation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed sharply to 29ns; IndexByte/subslice/helper overhead is worse than the existing single-pass numeric parser for short version strings.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry delimiter-search parsers for this workload. The current parser is already better for short stable versions.

description: fast path single-digit minor and patch in stable parser

asi.hypothesis: Many real stable release versions have single-digit minor and patch components; after parsing major, a conservative fast path for x.d.d(+metadata) can avoid the general minor/patch accumulation loops while preserving fallback for multi-digit components.
asi.why_it_helped: The latest workload version has single-digit minor/patch, so parseStableCore returns before the general minor and patch loops; the change is not feature-count-specific and still validates strict semver syntax.
asi.correctness_note: The fast path accepts any single digit 0-9 for minor and patch, with optional build metadata validation; multi-digit or prerelease inputs continue through existing general/fallback logic.
